internalName: Punyisa Pachira by Mary Pakamard, Mira Valley (Pa Khlok) — 4-bed villa, ฿123.026M
title: Architect-Designed 4–5 Bed Pool Villas — Mountain Views, Northeast Phuket
blurb: A small collection of newly built four- and five-bedroom pool villas set high in the forested hills of northeast Phuket, where gardens of koi ponds, natural swimming ponds and infinity edges look out over an unbroken panorama of jungle mountains and reservoir. Private plots of 1,468–3,100 m² wrap 427–680 m² of interior beneath soaring vaulted teak ceilings — four to five en-suite bedrooms, six to seven bathrooms and generous indoor-outdoor living, finished with handcrafted joinery and Phuket-heritage detailing throughout. Offered from ฿123,026,000 (about US$3.68M).
detail:
Set high in the green hills of northeast Phuket, this small collection of newly built villas occupies its own quiet plateau between forested mountains and a reservoir, with an outlook that runs uninterrupted across the valley. It is a genuinely private, low-density setting — yet the island's conveniences stay close: roughly 6 km to the Boat Avenue and Laguna lifestyle district, about 13.5 km to Layan Beach, and around 22 km to Phuket International Airport.

Each home sits on its own generous plot — from roughly 1,468 up to 3,100 m² of land, close to a full rai and beyond given over to a single villa, a deliberate choice that trades density for privacy, generous garden and an unbroken connection to the landscape. Rather than a conventional lawn, the grounds are conceived as a water garden: koi ponds threaded with stepping stones and a timber bridge, a natural-edged swimming pond, and a sculptural infinity pool that appears to spill toward the mountains beyond.

Architecturally the villas are a refined take on the tropical pavilion. Soaring vaulted ceilings clad in pale teak and framed by exposed beams give the main living spaces a sense of volume and calm, while walls of glass slide away to dissolve the line between inside and garden. Natural stone, warm timber and woven textures carry a resort-like ease through every room, and custom artworks and water features are woven into the composition rather than added to it.

Inside, an open great room flows into a dining space set for twelve and a designer kitchen with a fully fitted preparation area behind it. A separate study and library, a cellar-style bar and a series of shaded terraces extend the living space outward. Much of the joinery and furniture is bespoke — crafted to the design's own specification — lending the interiors a level of originality and finish rarely found in a production villa.

Depending on the type, each villa offers four or five en-suite bedrooms and six or seven bathrooms, arranged for privacy and each framing the view. The principal suite opens through full-height glazing to the mountains and pool, with a spa-like bathroom of Thai teak cabinetry, oak-lined ceilings and a freestanding tub set to the outlook — detailing that draws quietly on the craftsmanship of Phuket's colonial-era homes. Interior areas range from 427 to 680 m² across the collection.

Being offered ahead of completion, the homes can still be tailored: buyers are able to work with the design team on finishes and personal touches so each villa reflects how they intend to live in it. They are sold unfurnished, with furniture, fixtures and artwork available by separate negotiation.

The villas form part of a masterplanned, sustainability-led community — solar-powered systems and LEED-certified green zones set within a large tract of protected valley landscape. Offered from ฿123,026,000 (approximately US$3.68M). Message us to arrange details, floor plans and a viewing.

features.l: Private plots of 1,468–3,100 m² — one villa each
Infinity pool, natural pond & koi water garden
Soaring vaulted teak ceilings & exposed beams
6 km to Boat Avenue lifestyle hub
